message:
feat(cli): add ntm recipes command for managing session presets

Implements CLI access to the recipe system with list and show subcommands:

Recipe system overview:
- Recipes define reusable session configurations (agent types, counts, models)
- Three source levels with priority: project > user > builtin
- Project recipes: .ntm/recipes.toml (highest priority)
- User recipes: ~/.config/ntm/recipes.toml
- Built-in recipes: quick-claude, full-stack, minimal, codex-heavy, balanced

Commands:
- ntm recipes list: Show all available recipes with source and agents
- ntm recipes show <name>: Display detailed recipe configuration
- Both support --json output for scripting

List output:
- Groups recipes by source (builtin, user, project)
- Shows agent summary (e.g., "2 cc, 1 cod")
- Includes description for each recipe

Show output:
- Full recipe details including model/persona overrides
- Usage hint: "ntm spawn <session> --recipe=<name>"
- JSON output includes full AgentSpec array

JSON types:
- RecipesListResult: recipes array with RecipeInfo items
- RecipeShowResult: detailed recipe with agents array

Usage:
  ntm recipes list              # Human-readable grouped list
  ntm recipes list --json       # JSON for scripts
  ntm recipes show full-stack   # Show recipe details

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-84t","title":"Implement auto-restart for crashed agents","description":"Automatically restart agents that crash or exit unexpectedly.

## What This Task Does

Add --auto-restart flag to ntm spawn that monitors agents and restarts
them if they crash. This builds on the health check infrastructure.

## Interface

$ ntm spawn myproject --cc=2 --auto-restart
Starting agents with auto-restart enabled...
[Agent cc_1 crashed, restarting in 30s...]
[Agent cc_1 restarted successfully]

Configuration:
[resilience]
auto_restart = true
max_restarts = 3
restart_delay_seconds = 30

## Technical Approach

Option 1: Background goroutine
- Spawn launches a monitor goroutine
- Periodically checks health
- Restarts crashed agents
- Exits when session ends

Option 2: Separate daemon
- ntm spawn --auto-restart starts a background process
- Process monitors and restarts
- Survives terminal close

Recommendation: Start with Option 1 (simpler), evolve to Option 2 if needed.

## Restart Logic

1. Detect crash via health check (exit code != 0, process gone)
2. Check restart count for this agent
3. If under max_restarts:
   a. Wait restart_delay_seconds
   b. Get agent type from pane title
   c. Re-run agent command in same pane
   d. Increment restart count
4. If over max_restarts:
   a. Log failure
   b. Send notification (if enabled)
   c. Stop monitoring this agent

## State Tracking

Need to track per-agent:
- Restart count since session start
- Last restart time
- Original agent command

Store in:
- Memory (simple, lost on ntm exit)
- Tmux environment variable (persists)
- File (overkill for now)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] --auto-restart flag enables monitoring
- [ ] Crashed agents are detected and restarted
- [ ] Restart delay is configurable
- [ ] Max restart limit is enforced
- [ ] Restart count shown in health output
- [ ] Works with existing spawn command

 {"id":"ntm-8h3","title":"EPIC: Automation, Pipelines & Integration","description":"Advanced automation including agent pipelines, webhooks, and external integrations.

## Problem Statement
NTM is currently manual and reactive:
- Each prompt requires explicit user action
- No way to chain agent outputs as inputs to other agents
- No notifications when agents finish or error
- No integration with external tools (CI, Slack, etc.)
- File content must be manually copied into prompts

## Goals
1. Enable pipeline mode (chain agents sequentially)
2. Support webhook/notification on events
3. Easy file content injection into prompts
4. Token/cost estimation before sending expensive prompts

## Success Criteria
- `ntm pipeline` chains agent stages with output passing
- Config supports on_error, on_complete webhooks
- `ntm send --file=path` injects file content
- `ntm send --estimate` shows token/cost estimate

## Pipeline Mode
```bash
ntm pipeline myproject \
  --stage="cc: Design the authentication module" \
  --stage="cod: Implement the design from previous stage" \
  --stage="gmi: Write tests for the implementation"
```

Each stage:
1. Sends prompt to specified agent type
2. Waits for completion (detect idle state)
3. Captures output
4. Passes output as context to next stage

## Webhook Config
```toml
[notifications]
on_error = "curl -X POST https://slack.webhook/..."
on_complete = "terminal-notifier -message 'Done'"
on_rate_limit = "echo 'Rate limited' >> ~/ntm-alerts.log"
```

## File Context
```bash
ntm send myproject --all \
  --file=src/auth.py \
  --file=src/models.py:10-50 \
  "Refactor these files to use dependency injection"
```

Injects files with formatting:
```
# File: src/auth.py
\`\`\`python
<file contents>
\`\`\`
```

## Cost Estimation
- Count tokens in prompt using tiktoken or similar
- Estimate based on known pricing (Claude, Codex, Gemini)
- Show estimate, ask for confirmation on expensive prompts

## User Value
- Sophisticated multi-stage workflows
- Stay informed without constant monitoring
- Faster prompt construction with file injection
- Budget awareness for API-heavy usage","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:38:05.291885-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:38:05.291885-05:00"}
-{"id":"ntm-8h3.1","title":"File Context Injection (--file flag)","description":"Add --file flag to ntm send to inject file contents into prompts.

## Use Case
Instead of manually copying file contents, specify files to include:
```bash
ntm send myproject --all \
  --file=src/auth.py \
  --file=src/models.py \
  "Refactor these to use dependency injection"
```

## Prompt Injection Format
Files are prepended to prompt with clear formatting:
```
# File: src/auth.py
\`\`\`python
<contents of src/auth.py>
\`\`\`

# File: src/models.py
\`\`\`python
<contents of src/models.py>
\`\`\`

---

Refactor these to use dependency injection
```

## Extended Syntax
```bash
--file=path                    # Entire file
--file=path:10-50              # Lines 10-50
--file=path:10-                # Line 10 to end
--file=path:-50                # First 50 lines
--file=path:func:my_function   # Extract function (future)
```

## Implementation
```go
type FileSpec struct {
    Path      string
    StartLine int  // 0 = from beginning
    EndLine   int  // 0 = to end
}

func ParseFileSpec(spec string) (FileSpec, error) {
    // Parse "path:start-end" format
}

func InjectFiles(specs []FileSpec, prompt string) (string, error) {
    var parts []string
    
    for _, spec := range specs {
        content, err := readFileRange(spec)
        if err != nil {
            return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to read %s: %w", spec.Path, err)
        }
        
        lang := detectLanguage(spec.Path)
        header := fmt.Sprintf("# File: %s", spec.Path)
        if spec.StartLine > 0 || spec.EndLine > 0 {
            header += fmt.Sprintf(" (lines %d-%d)", spec.StartLine, spec.EndLine)
        }
        
        parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s\n```%s\n%s\n```", 
            header, lang, content))
    }
    
    parts = append(parts, "---\n\n"+prompt)
    return strings.Join(parts, "\n\n"), nil
}
```

## Language Detection
Map file extensions to markdown language tags:
```go
var langMap = map[string]string{
    ".py":   "python",
    ".go":   "go",
    ".js":   "javascript",
    ".ts":   "typescript",
    ".rs":   "rust",
    ".rb":   "ruby",
    ".java": "java",
    // etc.
}
```

## Integration with Templates
Templates can reference {{file}} and {{files}}:
```markdown
---
name: review
variables:
  file: required
---
Review the following file for issues:

{{file}}

Focus on security and performance.
```

## Flags
| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| -f, --file | File to include (repeatable) |
| --file-format | Format: markdown (default), raw, xml |
| --no-file-header | Omit file path headers |

## Edge Cases
- File not found: Error with clear message
- Binary files: Skip with warning
- Very large files: Warn if > 50KB, --force to override
- Relative vs absolute paths: Resolve relative to cwd

## Files to Modify
- internal/cli/send.go - Add --file flag processing
- internal/prompt/inject.go - File injection logic (new)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:58.519465-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:58.519465-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-8h3.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-8h3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:58.520574-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-8js","title":"Implement ntm personas list/show commands","description":"# Implement ntm personas list/show Commands

## Purpose

Allow users to discover and inspect available personas.

## Commands

```bash
# List all personas
ntm personas list
ntm personas list --json

# Show specific persona
ntm personas show architect
ntm personas show architect --json
```

## List Output

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Available Personas                                         │
├──────────────┬────────────┬────────────────────────────────┤
│ Name         │ Agent      │ Description                    │
├──────────────┼────────────┼────────────────────────────────┤
│ architect    │ claude     │ Senior software architect      │
│ implementer  │ claude     │ Fast implementation engineer   │
│ reviewer     │ claude     │ Meticulous code reviewer       │
│ tester       │ codex      │ Test engineer                  │
│ documenter   │ gemini     │ Documentation writer           │
├──────────────┴────────────┴────────────────────────────────┤
│ Source: 3 built-in, 2 user (~/.config/ntm/personas.toml)   │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Show Output

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Persona: architect                                         │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Agent Type:   claude                                       │
│ Model:        opus                                         │
│ Temperature:  0.7                                          │
│ Source:       built-in                                     │
│ Tags:         design, review                               │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ System Prompt:                                             │
│                                                            │
│ You are a senior software architect. Focus on:             │
│ - System design and high-level structure                   │
│ - Reviewing and guiding implementation                     │
│ - Identifying architectural risks                          │
│ - NOT writing implementation code (delegate that)          │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Context Files:                                             │
│ - ARCHITECTURE.md                                          │
│ - docs/design/*.md                                         │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## JSON Output

```json
{
  "name": "architect",
  "description": "Senior software architect",
  "agent_type": "claude",
  "model": "opus",
  "temperature": 0.7,
  "system_prompt": "...",
  "context_files": ["ARCHITECTURE.md", "docs/design/*.md"],
  "tags": ["design", "review"],
  "source": "built-in"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ntm personas list shows all personas
- [ ] ntm personas show displays details
- [ ] Source indicator (built-in, user, project)
- [ ] --json output for scripts
- [ ] Filtering by tag or agent type","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteStone","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:23:49.772792-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T04:39:02.092809-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T04:39:02.092809-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-8js","depends_on_id":"ntm-hzl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:24:01.048364-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-yxa","title":"Implement session agent registration","description":"# Implement Session Coordinator Registration

## Purpose

Register NTM as a COORDINATOR agent with Agent Mail - not as a replacement for the actual agents, but as the orchestration layer.

## IMPORTANT CLARIFICATION

NTM registers as a **coordinator**, not as the agents themselves:
- Identity: `ntm_<session>` (e.g., `ntm_myproject`)
- Program: `ntm`
- Role: Coordinator/orchestrator
- Purpose: User-initiated messages, session-level file reservations

The actual Claude/Codex/Gemini agents may have their own Agent Mail identities (e.g., `GreenCastle`, `BlueLake`). NTM doesn't impersonate them.

## Registration Flow

```go
func RegisterSessionCoordinator(client *agentmail.Client, sessionName, projectDir string) (*agentmail.Agent, error) {
    // 1. Ensure project exists
    if _, err := client.EnsureProject(projectDir); err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    // 2. Register coordinator identity
    return client.RegisterAgent(RegisterAgentInput{
        ProjectKey:      projectDir,
        Program:         "ntm",
        Model:           "coordinator",
        Name:            fmt.Sprintf("ntm_%s", sanitize(sessionName)),
        TaskDescription: "NTM session coordinator - user-initiated actions",
    })
}
```

## When Registration Happens

- **On spawn**: Register coordinator after session creation
- **On attach**: Update last_active_ts (agent already exists)
- **Optional**: Can skip if Agent Mail unavailable

## Discovering Other Agents

After registration, NTM can discover other agents in the project:
```go
agents, _ := client.ListAgents(projectDir)
// Returns: GreenCastle (claude-code), BlueLake (claude-code), RedStone (codex-cli), etc.
```

This enables the aggregate inbox view.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Coordinator registered as `ntm_<session>` on spawn
- [ ] Registration is idempotent (attach doesn't create duplicate)
- [ ] Other project agents discoverable via ListAgents
- [ ] Registration failures don't block session creation
- [ ] Clear distinction: NTM is coordinator, not the agents","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ChartreuseLake","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:18:03.476453-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:36:01.613618-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T03:36:01.613618-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-yxa","depends_on_id":"ntm-uwa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:55.757544-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-yyh","title":"Integration Test: status detection with real panes","description":"# Integration Test: Status Detection

## Scope
Verify agent status detection works with real tmux pane output.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Idle Detection
- Create pane with shell prompt
- Run status detection
- Verify detected as idle

### Scenario 2: Working Detection
- Create pane with active output
- Run status detection
- Verify detected as working

### Scenario 3: Error Detection
- Create pane with error message (rate limit, auth, etc.)
- Run status detection
- Verify error detected and classified

### Scenario 4: Agent Type Detection
- Create panes with different agent titles
- Run type detection
- Verify Claude, Codex, Gemini correctly identified

### Scenario 5: ANSI Handling
- Create pane with colored output
- Run detection
- Verify ANSI codes don't interfere

## Implementation
- tests/integration/status_test.go
- Create real tmux panes
- Send specific content via tmux send-keys
- Run detection functions
- Verify results","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:23:18.827555-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:23:18.827555-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-yyh","depends_on_id":"ntm-66c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:24:41.737081-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-yyh","depends_on_id":"ntm-7lk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:24:42.80065-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"ntm-z2k","title":"Update pane naming to include model identifier","description":"# Update Pane Naming to Include Variant Identifier

## Purpose

Enhance pane titles to include model OR persona information for identification and filtering.

## CRITICAL: Resolving Naming Conflict

Both Multi-Model and Personas want to add info to pane names. We need ONE consistent scheme.

**Decision: Use "variant" as a unified concept**
- Variant = persona name (if persona specified)
- Variant = model alias (if only model specified)
- Variant = empty (if neither, for backwards compatibility)

## Naming Format

`{session}__{type}_{index}[_{variant}]`

Examples:
```
myproject__cc_1              # No variant (default model, no persona)
myproject__cc_1_opus         # Model variant (--cc=1:opus)
myproject__cc_1_architect    # Persona variant (--persona=architect)
myproject__cod_2_gpt4        # Model variant
myproject__gmi_1_reviewer    # Persona variant
```

## Priority Rules

1. If --persona specified: Use persona name as variant
2. Else if model specified: Use model alias as variant
3. Else: No variant (backwards compatible)

Rationale: Persona implies model (persona includes model setting), so persona takes precedence.

## Parsing Logic

```go
// Regex: session__type_index[_variant]
var paneNameRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`^(.+)__(\w+)_(\d+)(?:_(\w+))?$`)

type PaneInfo struct {
    Session string
    Type    AgentType  // cc, cod, gmi
    Index   int
    Variant string     // model alias OR persona name, may be empty
}

func ParsePaneName(title string) (*PaneInfo, error) {
    matches := paneNameRegex.FindStringSubmatch(title)
    if matches == nil {
        return nil, ErrInvalidPaneName
    }
    return &PaneInfo{
        Session: matches[1],
        Type:    AgentType(matches[2]),
        Index:   parseInt(matches[3]),
        Variant: matches[4], // May be empty string
    }, nil
}
```

## Variant Type Detection

To know if variant is persona or model:
```go
func (p *PaneInfo) VariantType() VariantType {
    if p.Variant == "" {
        return VariantNone
    }
    if cfg.IsPersonaName(p.Variant) {
        return VariantPersona
    }
    return VariantModel
}
```

## Filtering Support

```bash
ntm send myproject --cc:opus "..."      # Filter by model
ntm send myproject --cc:architect "..." # Filter by persona
ntm send myproject --cc "..."           # All Claude agents
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Pane names include variant when persona or model specified
- [ ] Persona takes precedence over model for variant
- [ ] Backwards compatible with existing panes (no variant)
- [ ] ParsePaneName handles all formats
- [ ] Filtering works by variant (model or persona)
- [ ] Dashboard and status show variant info","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteMountain","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:21:43.467199-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T09:27:09.70184-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-z2k","depends_on_id":"ntm-057","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:21:53.966599-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

## CRITICAL: Resolving Naming Conflict

Both Multi-Model and Personas want to add info to pane names. We need ONE consistent scheme.

**Decision: Use "variant" as a unified concept**
- Variant = persona name (if persona specified)
- Variant = model alias (if only model specified)
- Variant = empty (if neither, for backwards compatibility)

## Naming Format

`{session}__{type}_{index}[_{variant}]`

Examples:
```
myproject__cc_1              # No variant (default model, no persona)
myproject__cc_1_opus         # Model variant (--cc=1:opus)
myproject__cc_1_architect    # Persona variant (--persona=architect)
myproject__cod_2_gpt4        # Model variant
myproject__gmi_1_reviewer    # Persona variant
```

## Priority Rules

1. If --persona specified: Use persona name as variant
2. Else if model specified: Use model alias as variant
3. Else: No variant (backwards compatible)

Rationale: Persona implies model (persona includes model setting), so persona takes precedence.

## Parsing Logic

```go
// Regex: session__type_index[_variant]
var paneNameRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`^(.+)__(\w+)_(\d+)(?:_(\w+))?$`)

type PaneInfo struct {
    Session string
    Type    AgentType  // cc, cod, gmi
    Index   int
    Variant string     // model alias OR persona name, may be empty
}

func ParsePaneName(title string) (*PaneInfo, error) {
    matches := paneNameRegex.FindStringSubmatch(title)
    if matches == nil {
        return nil, ErrInvalidPaneName
    }
    return &PaneInfo{
        Session: matches[1],
        Type:    AgentType(matches[2]),
        Index:   parseInt(matches[3]),
        Variant: matches[4], // May be empty string
    }, nil
}
```

## Variant Type Detection

To know if variant is persona or model:
```go
func (p *PaneInfo) VariantType() VariantType {
    if p.Variant == "" {
        return VariantNone
    }
    if cfg.IsPersonaName(p.Variant) {
        return VariantPersona
    }
    return VariantModel
}
```

## Filtering Support

```bash
ntm send myproject --cc:opus "..."      # Filter by model
ntm send myproject --cc:architect "..." # Filter by persona
ntm send myproject --cc "..."           # All Claude agents
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Pane names include variant when persona or model specified
- [ ] Persona takes precedence over model for variant
- [ ] Backwards compatible with existing panes (no variant)
- [ ] ParsePaneName handles all formats
- [ ] Filtering works by variant (model or persona)
- [ ] Dashboard and status show variant info","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteMountain","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:21:43.467199-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T09:29:28.223694-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T09:29:28.223694-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-z2k","depends_on_id":"ntm-057","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:21:53.966599-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-zh1e","title":"Add scanner configuration system (.ntm.yaml)","description":"## Overview
Create a configuration system for UBS scanner integration, allowing customization of scan behavior, thresholds, and tool selection.

## Background
Different projects have different needs - some want strict scanning, others more lenient. A configuration system enables:
- Per-project customization
- Consistent behavior across team
- Easy toggling of features

## Implementation Details

### Config File Location
Search order:
1. .ntm.yaml in project root
2. ~/.config/ntm/config.yaml (global defaults)
3. Built-in defaults

### Config Schema
```yaml
scanner:
  # UBS executable path (auto-detected if not set)
  ubs_path: ~/projects/ultimate_bug_scanner/ubs
  
  # Default scan settings
  defaults:
    timeout: 60s
    parallel: true
    exclude:
      - vendor/**
      - node_modules/**
      - "*.test.go"
  
  # Severity thresholds for different contexts
  thresholds:
    pre_commit:
      block_critical: true
      block_errors: 5
    ci:
      fail_critical: true
      fail_errors: 0
    dashboard:
      show_warnings: true
      show_info: false
  
  # Tool selection (which UBS tools to run)
  tools:
    enabled:
      - semgrep
      - gosec
      - staticcheck
    disabled:
      - bandit  # Python not used in this project
  
  # Auto-bead creation settings
  beads:
    auto_create: true
    min_severity: error
    auto_close: true
    
  # Notification settings
  notifications:
    enabled: true
    on_new_critical: true
    summary_after_scan: true
```

### Files to Create/Modify
- internal/config/scanner.go - Scanner config types
- internal/config/loader.go - Config loading logic
- internal/scanner/config.go - Config integration

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Config file parsed correctly
- [ ] Defaults applied when config missing
- [ ] Config validation with helpful errors
- [ ] Environment variable overrides supported","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:34:11.668449-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:38:43.48092-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-zh1e","depends_on_id":"ntm-p77j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:35:03.091094-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
